questions.question: The assassination of which historical figure in Sarajevo acted as the immediate spark that ignited World War I?
In what year did the United States enter World War I, following events like the sinking of the Lusitania and the Zimmermann Telegram?
Which treaty, signed in 1919, officially ended World War I but imposed harsh economic reparations and territorial losses on Germany?
What country did Nazi Germany invade on September 1, 1939, prompting Britain and France to declare war, marking the start of WWII?
What was the code name for the Allied invasion of Normandy on June 6, 1944 (D-Day)?
Which major turning-point battle on the Eastern Front (1942-1943) resulted in the destruction of the German 6th Army and halted Nazi expansion into the USSR?
The surprise military strike on which naval base prompted the United States to officially enter World War II?
Who was the Prime Minister of the United Kingdom throughout the majority of World War II?
What international peacekeeping organization was created immediately after World War II to replace the failed League of Nations?
Which dynamic military strategy, meaning 'lightning war,' did Germany use to rapidly conquer Poland, Denmark, Norway, and France?
